title: Add deploy script for multi-service rollout
description: Creates a deployment script that rolls out updated versions of the API gateway, user service, and order service in parallel.
diff:
diff --git a/scripts/deploy.sh b/scripts/deploy.sh
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..d7e8f9a
--- /dev/null
+++ b/scripts/deploy.sh
@@ -0,0 +1,18 @@
+#!/bin/bash
+set -e
+
+# Deploy all services in parallel for speed
+kubectl set image deployment/api-gateway api-gateway=$REGISTRY/api-gateway:$TAG &
+kubectl set image deployment/user-service user-service=$REGISTRY/user-service:$TAG &
+kubectl set image deployment/order-service order-service=$REGISTRY/order-service:$TAG &
+
+wait
+
+echo "All services deployed successfully"
comments: dana: Parallel deploy ignores service dependencies. The order-service depends on user-service v2 API. If order-service deploys before user-service, it will fail because it calls v2 endpoints that don't exist yet. Need ordered rollout: user-service first, then order-service.
sre-eric: Also no rollback on failure. If user-service fails to deploy, we're left in a partially updated state. Need canary or blue-green strategy.
author-fay: I'll add ordered deployment with health checks between stages.
author: fay
